name: Bruce Flett
title: Software Engineer
summary:
I don't just write code; I design systems that last. With over four decades of engineering experience spanning custom model engines, real-time distributed networks, and hardware-software integration, I excel at turning complex, ambiguous problems into clean, high-performance architecture.

Throughout my career, I’ve scaled enterprise caching layers, built bespoke multimedia automation pipelines, and experimented with everything from Dynamic Markov Models on edge hardware to modern metadata-driven servers utilizing Laravel, Vue, and Dart compiled to WASM. Whether architecting a real-time WebSocket infrastructure or optimizing a legacy codebase, I build with a focus on long-term maintainability, strict performance standards, and practical business utility.

My journey through software engineering has never been linear, and that’s exactly why I love it. I’ve written bare-metal custom frameworks with bespoke caching layers for major institutions, built custom WordPress engines that are still silently running over a decade later, and wired up Raspberry Pis to detect overhead drones using mathematical modeling.

I build tools that solve real problems. When my family needed to organize and process thousands of legacy photos, I built an automated orchestration asset manager. When I needed to collaborate seamlessly with a remote music producer, I engineered a custom Django audio-stem and asset synchronization hub. I look at software as an ever-evolving ecosystem—which is why today you’ll find me pushing the boundaries of the browser by compiling Dart to WebAssembly and wiring up real-time WebSocket pipelines.
